metadata.title: Task 4.11 EA-2: P1+P2 Security Hardening — Regex Normalization, Sensitivity Required, Monotonic Clock, Symlink Guard, Confidence Fail-Closed, ADR-012 §2.3 Cleanup
metadata.test_baseline: 742/744 passed in `pytest shared/ services/` scope (2 pre-existing: test_build_prompt_does_not_contain_no_think, test_stop_token_ids_constants_defined)
metadata.scope_limit: 5 production files + 1 ADR doc + tests. All items are small, surgical changes.

file.reason: RECON-1 sensitivity audit — EA-1 output confirming minimal test impact
DPC regex + confidence fallback (P1-1, P2-3 target)
Config path resolution — symlink check (P2-2 target)

role_constraints.role: Execution Agent — implements exactly the scoped milestone below.
role_constraints.constraint: Windows 11 host, PowerShell terminal, Python venv at `.venv/`.
Git: create branch `feature/p5-task4-11-security-ea2` from commit 13e4173 (EA-1 HEAD on feature/p5-task4-11-security-ea1) before any edits.

comprehension_gate.instruction:
Before writing ANY code, creating branches, or running commands, you MUST: 1. Read this entire prompt. 2. Present a structured summary to the Lead Architect containing: (a) Milestone objective — in your own words. (b) Work items — list each with a 1-sentence description. (c) Files you will modify (production + test). (d) Test strategy — what you will test and how. (e) Risks or ambiguities you see. 3. STOP and WAIT for explicit approval from the Lead Architect. 4. Only after receiving approval, proceed with execution_order step 1.
This gate exists so the Lead Architect can verify you understood the task correctly before any changes are made. Do NOT skip or abbreviate this step.

milestone.title: P1+P2 Security Hardening + Doc Cleanup
milestone.objective: Close all remaining P1 and P2 findings from docs/SECURITY_ASSESSMENT.md, plus clean up the stale ADR-012 §2.3 Draft-B row. This completes Task 4.11 in its entirety, unblocking Task 5 (Model Upgrade to Qwen3-14B).

work_item.title: Authority Claim Regex Unicode Normalization
work_item.description: `DeterministicPolicyChecker._AUTHORITY_CLAIM_RE` at gpu_inference.py ~L330 has 5 narrow patterns. Unicode homoglyphs (e.g., U+2011 non-breaking hyphen instead of ASCII `-`, U+FF41 fullwidth 'a' instead of ASCII 'a') bypass the regex entirely. Since this rule defends against social engineering in `parameters_schema`, its bypass enables prompt injection payloads that evade the DPC and reach the LLM.
work_item.current_state: ```python _AUTHORITY_CLAIM_RE: ClassVar[re.Pattern[str]] = re.compile( r"pre-approved|pre_approved(?!_)|security.{0,5}team.{0,10}author|authorization.{0,10}bypass|bypass.{0,10}author", re.IGNORECASE, ) ``` No Unicode normalization applied before matching.

change.description: Add NFKD normalization before regex match

required_changes.change: 1. Import `unicodedata` at the top of the file. 2. In `DeterministicPolicyChecker.check()`, immediately before the `cls._AUTHORITY_CLAIM_RE.search(params_str)` call (~RULE 4), normalize the search string: `params_normalized = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", params_str)`. 3. Search the regex against `params_normalized` instead of `params_str`. 4. NFKD collapses compatibility equivalences (fullwidth → ASCII, ligatures → components, non-breaking hyphen → regular hyphen). This closes the homoglyph bypass vector. 5. Optionally: also apply NFKD to the exfiltration check (RULE 2 `/tmp/export/`) for consistency — same 1-line pattern.

test.description: Unit test: DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM fires on Unicode homoglyph bypass

test_requirements.test: Construct a CAR with `parameters_schema={"note": "pre\u2011approved"}` (U+2011 non-breaking hyphen). Verify check() returns ("DENY", "DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM").

test.description: Unit test: DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM fires on fullwidth characters

test_requirements.test: Construct a CAR with `parameters_schema={"note": "\uff50\uff52\uff45-approved"}` (fullwidth "pre"). Verify check() returns ("DENY", "DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM").

test.description: Regression: existing DENY_AUTHORITY_CLAIM ASCII tests still pass

test_requirements.test: All pre-existing authority claim tests must pass unchanged.

work_item.title: Sensitivity Required Field
work_item.description:
`sensitivity` defaults to `Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED` in both the Pydantic model (shared/schemas/car.py L93) and the `build_car()` factory (services/policy_agent/src/car.py L33). While the rule engine correctly DENY-blocks UNCLASSIFIED CARs (fail-closed), the silent default masks integration bugs. Making `sensitivity` required catches errors at construction time instead of adjudication time.
Per RECON-1 (docs/P5_TASK4_11_SENSITIVITY_RECON.md): ALL test helpers already explicitly set `sensitivity`. Only 1 test (`test_build_car_unclassified_sensitivity_default` in test_car.py) validates the default behavior itself — that test must be rewritten.
work_item.current_state:
```python # shared/schemas/car.py L93-95 sensitivity: Sensitivity = Field( default=Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED, description="Declared payload sensitivity. UNCLASSIFIED triggers Fail-Closed.", )
# services/policy_agent/src/car.py L33 sensitivity: Sensitivity | str = Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED, ```

required_changes.change: Change from: ```python sensitivity: Sensitivity = Field( default=Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED, description="Declared payload sensitivity. UNCLASSIFIED triggers Fail-Closed.", ) ``` To: ```python sensitivity: Sensitivity = Field( ..., description="Declared payload sensitivity. Required — callers must be explicit.", ) ```

Change the `build_car()` signature from: ```python sensitivity: Sensitivity | str = Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED, ``` To: ```python sensitivity: Sensitivity | str, ``` Move the `sensitivity` parameter BEFORE `parameters_schema` (which has a default) to maintain valid Python signature ordering (required params before optional params).

change.description: Rewrite the default-test to test ValidationError

required_changes.change: Replace `test_build_car_unclassified_sensitivity_default`: - OLD: constructs a CAR without sensitivity and asserts it equals UNCLASSIFIED. - NEW: constructs a CAR without sensitivity and asserts it raises `ValidationError` (from Pydantic) or equivalent construction error. Also: verify that explicitly passing `sensitivity=Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED` still works (the value is valid, it just can't be the default).

test.description: Unit test: omitting sensitivity raises ValidationError

test_requirements.test: Attempt to construct CanonicalActionRepresentation without sensitivity. Verify raises ValidationError.

test.description: Unit test: explicit UNCLASSIFIED still accepted

test_requirements.test: Construct CanonicalActionRepresentation with sensitivity=Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED. Verify it succeeds (value is valid, just not default).

test.description: Unit test: build_car() without sensitivity raises TypeError

test_requirements.test: Call build_car() without the sensitivity argument. Verify raises TypeError (missing required positional argument).

test.description: Regression: all other tests pass unchanged

test_requirements.test: RECON-1 confirmed all helpers explicitly set sensitivity. Expect zero cascading failures.

work_item.title: NonceStore Monotonic Clock
work_item.description: NonceStore in jwt_validator.py uses `time.time()` at two sites (check_and_add L80 and _gc L90). If the system clock jumps backward (NTP correction), nonces persist past their TTL. The rate limiter already uses `time.monotonic()` correctly.
work_item.current_state:
```python # jwt_validator.py check_and_add(): self._nonces[nonce] = time.time()
# jwt_validator.py _gc(): cutoff = time.time() - self._ttl ```

required_changes.change: Two substitutions: 1. `self._nonces[nonce] = time.time()` → `self._nonces[nonce] = time.monotonic()` 2. `cutoff = time.time() - self._ttl` → `cutoff = time.monotonic() - self._ttl`

test.description: Existing NonceStore tests must pass unchanged

test_requirements.test: The switch from time.time() to time.monotonic() is semantically transparent to tests that use real sleeps or mock time. Verify no regressions.

work_item.title: Config Symlink Check
work_item.description: `resolve_service_config_path()` in runtime_config.py resolves paths via `.resolve()` but never checks if the result is a symlink. On a single-user system this requires local FS write access to exploit, but the fix is trivial.
work_item.current_state: ```python # runtime_config.py ~L143-146 if not resolved.exists(): raise ConfigResolutionError( code="CFG_PATH_MISSING", message=f"Config file not found: {resolved}", ) return resolved ``` No symlink check before returning.

change.description: Add symlink guard before exists check

required_changes.change: Insert BEFORE the `resolved.exists()` check: ```python if resolved.is_symlink(): raise ConfigResolutionError( code="CFG_SYMLINK_REJECTED", message=f"Config path is a symlink (rejected for security): {resolved}", ) ```

test.description: Unit test: symlink config path raises CFG_SYMLINK_REJECTED

test_requirements.test: Create a temporary symlink pointing to a valid config file. Call resolve_service_config_path with the symlink path. Verify raises ConfigResolutionError with code "CFG_SYMLINK_REJECTED".

test.description: Regression: normal (non-symlink) path resolution still works

test_requirements.test: Existing resolve_service_config_path tests must pass unchanged.

work_item.title: Confidence Fallback Fail-Closed
work_item.description: `_DEFAULT_LABEL_CONFIDENCE` at gpu_inference.py ~L89-92 returns 0.995 for all labels when the confidence regex fails to match. This means garbled LLM output gets treated as high-confidence. While no threshold gate exists yet, this is latent fail-open risk.

required_changes.change: Change all values to 0.0: ```python _DEFAULT_LABEL_CONFIDENCE: dict[str, float] = { "ALLOW": 0.0, "DENY": 0.0, "ESCALATE": 0.0, } ``` This means: "the label was parsed but confidence is unknown." The label itself still drives the decision. The 0.0 confidence signals to any future threshold gate that this result was not explicitly confirmed by the model.

test.description: Unit test: verify fallback confidence is 0.0 for all labels
Regression: tests asserting specific confidence values need updating

test_requirements.test: Any test that asserts `confidence == 0.995` for a fallback case must be updated to assert `confidence == 0.0`. This is expected — it's the whole point of the fix.

work_item.title: ADR-012 §2.3 Draft-B Status Correction
work_item.description: ADR-012 §2.3 Draft Model Candidates table shows Draft-B (Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B) with status "ACQUIRING — P5-005c in progress". Task 4.2 already eliminated Draft-B. The status must be corrected.
work_item.current_state: ```markdown | Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B | 22 | 1024 | INT8_ASYM | ~300 MB (est.) | **ACQUIRING** — P5-005c in progress. 6 layers pruned from 0.6B, fine-tuned against 8B. Repo: `OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov` | ```

required_changes.change: Change the Status column of the Draft-B row from: `**ACQUIRING** — P5-005c in progress. 6 layers pruned from 0.6B, fine-tuned against 8B. Repo: \`OpenVINO/Qwen3-pruned-6L-from-0.6B-int8-ov\`` To: `**ELIMINATED** — P5-005c: pruned draft model not viable (see P5-005c evidence). Full 28L INT4 (Draft-A) confirmed as production draft.`

The paragraph after the table says: "Draft model selection will be locked once P5-005c benchmarking produces comparative evidence (pruned 22L INT8 vs full 28L INT4 at equivalent NAT values)."
Replace with: "Draft model selection LOCKED: Qwen3-0.6B 28L INT4 (Draft-A) is the production draft model. Draft-B (pruned 22L INT8_ASYM) eliminated in P5-005c."

execution_order.step: Checkout: `git checkout feature/p5-task4-11-security-ea1` then `git checkout -b feature/p5-task4-11-security-ea2`
Implement P1-1 (Unicode normalization in DPC regex). Write tests. Run.
Implement P1-2 (sensitivity required). Update test_car.py. Run tests — verify RECON-1 prediction (zero cascading failures).
Implement P2-1 (NonceStore monotonic clock). Run tests.
Implement P2-2 (config symlink guard). Write test. Run tests.
Implement P2-3 (confidence fallback to 0.0). Update affected tests. Run tests.
Run full test suite: `pytest shared/ services/ --tb=short -q`. Verify ≥ 742 passed (EA-1 baseline), zero new failures.
Update docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §1.22 — add EA-2 deliverables, update disposition to COMPLETE (full Task 4.11).

execution_prompt.commit_template:
``` security: Task 4.11 EA-2 — P1+P2 hardening + ADR-012 §2.3 cleanup
P1-1: DPC authority claim regex — NFKD Unicode normalization before match P1-2: sensitivity field — removed default, now required in CAR + build_car() P2-1: NonceStore — time.time() → time.monotonic() (clock-jump resilience) P2-2: resolve_service_config_path — symlink guard (CFG_SYMLINK_REJECTED) P2-3: confidence fallback — 0.995 → 0.0 (fail-closed for future threshold gates) DOC-1: ADR-012 §2.3 — Draft-B status corrected to ELIMINATED
Task 4.11 COMPLETE — all 7 SECURITY_ASSESSMENT.md findings closed. Task 5 (Model Upgrade to Qwen3-14B) unblocked.
Branch: feature/p5-task4-11-security-ea2 Gate: pytest shared/ services/ --tb=short -q — no regression from 742 baseline LEDGER: Entry 14 (P5-TASK4.11-EA2) ```

quality_gate.gate: All new and updated tests pass.

`pytest shared/ services/ --tb=short -q` — passed count ≥ 742 (EA-1 baseline). The 2 pre-existing failures (test_build_prompt_does_not_contain_no_think, test_stop_token_ids_constants_defined) are NOT regressions.
After P1-2 implementation, verify RECON-1 prediction: zero cascading test failures from removing the sensitivity default (all helpers already explicit).
EA-1 changes (CN validation, schema allowlist, boundary markers) must remain intact. Do NOT modify EA-1 deliverables except the confidence fallback dict in gpu_inference.py.

command.description: Verify changed files from EA-1 base

verification.command: python -m py_compile services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py; python -m py_compile shared/schemas/car.py; python -m py_compile services/policy_agent/src/car.py; python -m py_compile shared/crypto/jwt_validator.py; python -m py_compile shared/runtime_config.py

python -c "from shared.schemas.car import CanonicalActionRepresentation; CanonicalActionRepresentation(source_agent='x', destination_service='y', verb='READ', resource='z')" 2>&1 | findstr /I "sensitivity"

context_notes.note: When removing the `sensitivity` default from `build_car()`, the parameter must move BEFORE `parameters_schema` (which has `default=None`). Python requires non-default parameters before default parameters. The new order: `build_car(source_agent, destination_service, verb, resource, sensitivity, parameters_schema=None, session_id="")`

Apply NFKD normalization to `params_str` once, before BOTH the exfiltration check (RULE 2) and the authority claim check (RULE 4). This way both rules benefit from homoglyph collapse with only one normalization call.
Tests that assert `confidence == 0.995` for fallback scenarios will fail. Search for `0.995` in test files to find them. These are expected updates, not regressions.

`os.symlink()` on Windows may require elevated privileges or Developer Mode enabled. If creating a symlink in the test fails with PermissionError, wrap the test with `@pytest.mark.skipif(not can_symlink, reason="symlink not available")` where `can_symlink` is determined by a try/except probe at module scope.

gpu_inference.py was modified by EA-1 (schema allowlist, boundary markers, system prompt). EA-2 modifies the SAME file but DIFFERENT sections: _AUTHORITY_CLAIM_RE regex usage (~L355), _DEFAULT_LABEL_CONFIDENCE dict (~L89). No conflict expected.
After EA-2 commits, Task 4.11 is COMPLETE. Both EA branches need merging to main. The Lead Architect will handle the merge — do NOT merge to main.
